response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and embedded software developer with a background in web technologies, mobile, and low-latency systems, but has no demonstrated expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or AI accelerator performance optimization — the core competencies required for this Principal Performance Architect role. Their AI exposure is limited to application-level agentic tooling (MCP code generators), not hardware-level deep learning workload analysis or hardware-software co-design. Two Bachelor's degrees meet the minimum educational bar only if paired with 8+ years of relevant experience, which the candidate does not have in this domain.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency and performance optimization work using DLang with async I/O, fibers, and caching in a high-throughput microservices environment
Hardware-adjacent experience through robotic arm development and C/C++ work on NI LabWindows instrumentation software, showing some comfort below the application layer
Long tenure as a Staff/Senior IC with consistent technical leadership, independent project ownership, and open-source contributions demonstrating engineering depth
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the foundational technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from the resume
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or deep neural network training and inference optimization at the hardware or system level
AI experience is exclusively software-layer agent tooling (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows), with no connection to hardware accelerator workloads or performance/power trade-off analysis

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or chip architecture background
No deep learning workload or GPU/AI accelerator expertise
